$comment: Schema for src/data/projects.json. Stable registry fields are manually editable. Volatile fields must NOT be manually edited — they are populated by automation (see issue #3) or left null.

id.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. Unique slug matching the GitHub repo name.

name.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. Display name of the project.

role.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. One of: ui | runtime | agents | workflow

layer.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. Human-readable layer name.

description.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. One-paragraph project description.

repo.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. Full GitHub repository URL.

techStack.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. List of technologies used.

dependencies.$comment: STABLE — manually editable. IDs of other ecosystem projects this one depends on.

status.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Populated from GitHub Topics (issue #7). Leave null until automation is in place.

version.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Populated from GitHub Releases API latest tag_name (issue #3). Leave null until automation is in place.

lastUpdate.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Populated from GitHub API pushed_at (issue #3). Leave null until automation is in place.

openIssues.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Populated from GitHub API open_issues_count (issue #3). Leave null until automation is in place.

docs.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Populated from GitHub API repo.homepage (issue #8). Leave null until automation is in place.

prd.$comment: VOLATILE — do not manually edit. Strategy TBD (issue #8). Leave null until automation is in place.
